title: The Research Problem and Research Question - Comprehensive Quiz

questions.question: Chapter I of the research paper is called:
options: The Problem and Its Introduction (or background)

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of Chapter 1?
The components of Chapter 1 include: Introduction, Theoretical Framework, _____, Statement of the Problem, Significance of the Study, Scope and Limitations, and Definition of Terms.
The purpose of the introduction includes all of the following EXCEPT:
options: The general definition of the topic/problem area
Historical basis for the existence of the problem
Efforts made towards addressing similar challenges

questions.question: In the introduction, describing the existence of an unsatisfactory condition or a problem that needs a solution is called:
Giving the historical background of the problem, if applicable, is part of the:
Describing the geographical location of the study is part of the:
options: Geographical conditions of the research locale

questions.question: Giving the reason(s) why the study should be conducted is called the:
The introduction should describe the existence of an unsatisfactory condition or a problem that needs a solution. This is called the _____ of the problem.
The rationale of the study gives the _____ why the study should be conducted.
Which of the following is a 'DO' in writing a research introduction?
options: Explain the importance of your research topic
Forget the reader may not be familiar with details

questions.question: Which of the following is a 'DON'T' in writing a research introduction?
options: Focus on relevant aspects of your study
Engage readers by building a story

questions.question: In the do's and don'ts table, what should you do regarding missing links and gaps?
options: Talk about the main developments and missing links and gaps in your research area

questions.question: In the do's and don'ts table, what is a common mistake regarding the length of the background?
options: Writing a background that is too long or too short
Writing a background with clear structure
Writing a background with all details

questions.question: In the do's and don'ts table, what should you NOT forget to provide?
In the do's and don'ts table, what should you do with your findings?
options: Engage your findings in a chronological order
Only mention them in the conclusion

questions.question: In the do's and don'ts table, what should you NOT forget to check?
options: Your target journal's instructions for presenting the study background

questions.question: According to the do's and don'ts, what should you do to engage readers?
options: Build a story around the central theme of your research

questions.question: In the do's and don'ts, one should _____ ideas in a clear and concise manner.
In the do's and don'ts, one should NOT be _____ in writing, disorganized, or discuss unrelated subjects.
A theoretical framework is defined as a foundational review of existing theories that serves as a _____ for developing the arguments you will use in your own work.
A theoretical framework can be considered a _____ that is borrowed by researchers to develop their own research inquiry.
A theoretical framework is a set of concepts, theories, ideas, and assumptions that help you understand a specific:
A theoretical framework is a foundational review of existing _____ that serves as a roadmap for developing arguments in your own work.
A theoretical framework can be defined as a set of concepts, theories, ideas, and _____ that help you understand a specific phenomenon or problem.
A conceptual framework sets forth the standards to define a research question and find appropriate, meaningful answers for the:
A conceptual framework connects the theories, assumptions, beliefs, and _____ behind your research.
A conceptual framework sets forth the _____ to define a research question and find appropriate, meaningful answers.
A conceptual framework connects the theories, assumptions, beliefs, and concepts behind your research. It helps to define a research question and find:
The schematic diagram is also known as the:
The schematic diagram shows the relationship between the:
Figure 1 in the PDF is labeled as:
options: Figure 1. Schematic presentation between the independent and dependent variables

questions.question: Chapter 2 of the research paper is called:
options: Review of Related Literature and Studies

questions.question: Chapter 3 of the research paper is called:
options: Review of Related Literature and Studies

questions.question: The SIFT Method is mentioned in the PDF but not explained. Based on context, SIFT is likely a method for _____.
Which of the following is a component of Chapter 3 (Research Methodology)?
Which of the following is NOT a component of Chapter 3?
According to the do's and don'ts, what should you do regarding the importance of your research topic?
options: Assume the reader knows its importance

questions.question: According to the do's and don'ts, what should you NOT do regarding the reader?
options: Forget the reader may not be familiar with details

questions.question: What is the purpose of the introduction according to the PDF?
options: To provide the general definition of the topic/problem area, historical basis, efforts made, and why they failed
